channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B

item.title: Spin-Seebeck signatures of spin chirality in kagome antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

Noncollinear antiferromagnets (NCAFMs) are appealing for antiferromagnetic spintronics, as they combine the advantages of collinear antiferromagnets with novel emergent phenomena stemming from their complex spin structures. These phenomena are often associated with the intrinsic spin chirality, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014433] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

Noncollinear antiferromagnets (NCAFMs) are appealing for antiferromagnetic spintronics, as they combine the advantages of collinear antiferromagnets with novel emergent phenomena stemming from their complex spin structures. These phenomena are often associated with the intrinsic spin chirality, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014433] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Seebeck signatures of spin chirality in kagome antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Manipulating non-Abelian anyons of Kitaev quantum spin liquids with local magnetic fields
item.description:
Author(s): Haoran Wang and Alessandro Principi

Non-Abelian anyonic excitations of quantum spin liquids have potential for application to topological quantum computation, but designing logical operations requires developing protocols to faithfully create, move, and read out such quasiparticles. In this paper, we test two such protocols for manipu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014434] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haoran Wang and Alessandro Principi

Non-Abelian anyonic excitations of quantum spin liquids have potential for application to topological quantum computation, but designing logical operations requires developing protocols to faithfully create, move, and read out such quasiparticles. In this paper, we test two such protocols for manipu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014434] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Manipulating non-Abelian anyons of Kitaev quantum spin liquids with local magnetic fields
item.dc:creator: Haoran Wang and Alessandro Principi

item.title: Topological pump and its plateau transitions of an $N$-leg spin ladder
item.description:
Author(s): Kota Yamamoto, Yoshihito Kuno, Tomonari Mizoguchi, Kazuki Sone, and Yasuhiro Hatsugai

A topological pump on an $N\text{−}\mathrm{leg}$ spin ladder is discussed by introducing spatial clusterization whose adiabatic limit is a set of $2N\text{−}\mathrm{site}$ staircase clusters. We define a pump path in parameter space that connects two distinct symmetry-protected topological phases. B…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024432] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kota Yamamoto, Yoshihito Kuno, Tomonari Mizoguchi, Kazuki Sone, and Yasuhiro Hatsugai

A topological pump on an N−leg spin ladder is discussed by introducing spatial clusterization whose adiabatic limit is a set of 2N−site staircase clusters. We define a pump path in parameter space that connects two distinct symmetry-protected topological phases. By introducing a symmetry breaking st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024432] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological pump and its plateau transitions of an $N$-leg spin ladder
item.dc:creator: Kota Yamamoto, Yoshihito Kuno, Tomonari Mizoguchi, Kazuki Sone, and Yasuhiro Hatsugai

item.title: Multiple magnetic transitions, metamagnetism, and topological Hall effect in the rare-earth based plumbide GdRhPb
item.description:
Author(s): Rajesh Swami, Sonali S Pradhan, V. Kanchana, and Z. Hossain

We present a comprehensive investigation of the physical properties of a candidate nodal-line semimetal GdRhPb single crystals, through magnetization, heat capacity, and magnetotransport measurements. Temperature-dependent magnetization $[χ(T)]$ along different crystallographic directions confirm th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rajesh Swami, Sonali S Pradhan, V. Kanchana, and Z. Hossain

We present a comprehensive investigation of the physical properties of a candidate nodal-line semimetal GdRhPb single crystals, through magnetization, heat capacity, and magnetotransport measurements. Temperature-dependent magnetization [χ(T)] along different crystallographic directions confirm that…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Multiple magnetic transitions, metamagnetism, and topological Hall effect in the rare-earth based plumbide GdRhPb

item.title: Elementary building blocks for cluster Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Vaishnavi Jayakumar and Ciarán Hickey

Mott insulators, in which strong Coulomb interactions fully localize electrons on single atomic sites, play host to an incredibly rich and exciting array of strongly correlated physics. One can naturally extend this concept to cluster Mott insulators, wherein electrons localize not on single atoms b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vaishnavi Jayakumar and Ciarán Hickey

Mott insulators, in which strong Coulomb interactions fully localize electrons on single atomic sites, play host to an incredibly rich and exciting array of strongly correlated physics. One can naturally extend this concept to cluster Mott insulators, wherein electrons localize not on single atoms b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Two-site entanglement in the two-dimensional Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Frederic Bippus, Anna Kauch, Gergő Roósz, Christian Mayrhofer, Fakher Assaad, and Karsten Held

The study of entanglement in strongly correlated electron systems typically requires knowledge of the reduced density matrix. Here, we apply the parquet dynamical vertex approximation to study the two-site reduced density matrix at varying distance, in the Hubbard model at weak coupling. This allows…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Frederic Bippus, Anna Kauch, Gergő Roósz, Christian Mayrhofer, Fakher Assaad, and Karsten Held

The study of entanglement in strongly correlated electron systems typically requires knowledge of the reduced density matrix. Here, we apply the parquet dynamical vertex approximation to study the two-site reduced density matrix at varying distance, in the Hubbard model at weak coupling. This allows…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-site entanglement in the two-dimensional Hubbard model

item.title: Statistics of Abelian topological excitations
item.description:
Author(s): Hanyu Xue

In this paper, we develop a novel theory that generalizes the concept of anyon statistics to Abelian topological excitations of any dimension. We axiomatize excitations as a selected collection of states and operators satisfying the configuration axiom and the locality axiom, purely based on many-bo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045143] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hanyu Xue

In this paper, we develop a novel theory that generalizes the concept of anyon statistics to Abelian topological excitations of any dimension. We axiomatize excitations as a selected collection of states and operators satisfying the configuration axiom and the locality axiom, purely based on many-bo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045143] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Spin susceptibility in a pseudogap state with fluctuating spiral magnetic order
item.description:
Author(s): Paulo Forni, Pietro M. Bonetti, Henrik Müller-Groeling, Demetrio Vilardi, and Walter Metzner

We compute the electron spin susceptibility in the pseudogap regime of the two-dimensional Hubbard model in the framework of a SU(2) gauge theory of fluctuating magnetic order. The electrons are fractionalized in fermionic chargons with a pseudospin degree of freedom and bosonic spinons. The chargon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045144] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Paulo Forni, Pietro M. Bonetti, Henrik Müller-Groeling, Demetrio Vilardi, and Walter Metzner

We compute the electron spin susceptibility in the pseudogap regime of the two-dimensional Hubbard model in the framework of a SU(2) gauge theory of fluctuating magnetic order. The electrons are fractionalized in fermionic chargons with a pseudospin degree of freedom and bosonic spinons. The chargon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045144] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Quantum geometry from the Moyal product: Quantum kinetic equation and nonlinear response
item.description:
Author(s): Takamori Park, Xiaoyang Huang, Lucile Savary, and Leon Balents

We systematically derive the dissipationless quantum kinetic equation for a multiband free fermionic system with $U$(1) symmetry. Using the Moyal product formalism, we fully band diagonalize the dynamics. Expanding to the second order in gradients, which is beyond the semiclassical limit, we give a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045146] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Takamori Park, Xiaoyang Huang, Lucile Savary, and Leon Balents

We systematically derive the dissipationless quantum kinetic equation for a multiband free fermionic system with U(1) symmetry. Using the Moyal product formalism, we fully band diagonalize the dynamics. Expanding to the second order in gradients, which is beyond the semiclassical limit, we give a co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045146] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Optical study on charge density waves in Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome metals
item.description:
Author(s): Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang

Many studies on ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ doped with various dopants have been conducted to explore the relationship between charge density waves (CDWs) and superconductivity. Among them, only Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}\phantom{\rule{4pt}{0ex}}[\mathrm{Cs}{({\mathrm{V}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045147] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang

Many studies on CsV3Sb5 doped with various dopants have been conducted to explore the relationship between charge density waves (CDWs) and superconductivity. Among them, only Cr-doped CsV3Sb5[Cs(V1−xCrx)3Sb5] exhibits a simultaneous weakening of the CDW and superconductivity as the doping concentrat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045147] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical study on charge density waves in Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome metals

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Electronic crystal phases in the presence of nonuniform Berry curvature and tunable Berry flux: The ${λ}_{N}$-jellium model
item.description:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Joe Huxford, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, and Yong Baek Kim

The authors introduce here a continuum model with a tunable Berry curvature profile and total Berry flux, to study electronic crystallization in geometrically nontrivial bands. Their numerical study of the model reveals sharp distinctions between topology driven by an underlying periodic potential and topology arising from spontaneous crystallization under strong electronic interactions. They shed light on this contrasting behavior using analytical arguments, including a general weak-potential formula for the Chern number and energetic considerations for anomalous Hall and halo Wigner crystals.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045148] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Joe Huxford, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, and Yong Baek Kim

The authors introduce here a continuum model with a tunable Berry curvature profile and total Berry flux, to study electronic crystallization in geometrically nontrivial bands. Their numerical study of the model reveals sharp distinctions between topology driven by an underlying periodic potential and topology arising from spontaneous crystallization under strong electronic interactions. They shed light on this contrasting behavior using analytical arguments, including a general weak-potential formula for the Chern number and energetic considerations for anomalous Hall and halo Wigner crystals.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045148] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic crystal phases in the presence of nonuniform Berry curvature and tunable Berry flux: The ${λ}_{N}$-jellium model
item.dc:creator: Félix Desrochers, Joe Huxford, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, and Yong Baek Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Topological phase transitions and quarter-metal states of rhombohedral trilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-Sen Jin, Xi-Yue Wang, and Hang Xie

Rhombohedral trilayer graphene (RTG) hosts exotic electronic, magnetic, and superconductivity properties, offering a versatile platform to study correlated and topological phases. We systematically investigate topological phase transitions in RTG under optical fields and staggered electric potential…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao-Sen Jin, Xi-Yue Wang, and Hang Xie

Rhombohedral trilayer graphene (RTG) hosts exotic electronic, magnetic, and superconductivity properties, offering a versatile platform to study correlated and topological phases. We systematically investigate topological phase transitions in RTG under optical fields and staggered electric potential…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Chirality reversal at finite magnetic impurity strength and local signatures of a topological phase transition
item.description:
Author(s): Ruiqi Xu, Arnab Seth, and Itamar Kimchi

We study the honeycomb lattice with a single magnetic impurity modeled by adding imaginary next-nearest-neighbor hopping $ih$ on a single hexagon. This Haldane defect gives a topological mass term to the gapless Dirac cones and generates chirality. For a small density of defects, Neehus et al. [Phys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruiqi Xu, Arnab Seth, and Itamar Kimchi

We study the honeycomb lattice with a single magnetic impurity modeled by adding imaginary next-nearest-neighbor hopping ih on a single hexagon. This Haldane defect gives a topological mass term to the gapless Dirac cones and generates chirality. For a small density of defects, Neehus et al. [Phys. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Chirality reversal at finite magnetic impurity strength and local signatures of a topological phase transition

item.title: Charge current and phase diagram of the disordered open longer-range Kitaev chain
item.description:
Author(s): Emmanuele G. Cinnirella, Andrea Nava, and Domenico Giuliano

We compute the disorder averaged dc conductance in the non-equilibrium steady state that sets in between a longer-range Kitaev chain and a lead connected to an external reservoir, as a function of the system parameters and of the disorder strength. From our results, we map out the phase diagram of t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Emmanuele G. Cinnirella, Andrea Nava, and Domenico Giuliano

We compute the disorder averaged dc conductance in the non-equilibrium steady state that sets in between a longer-range Kitaev chain and a lead connected to an external reservoir, as a function of the system parameters and of the disorder strength. From our results, we map out the phase diagram of t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Charge current and phase diagram of the disordered open longer-range Kitaev chain
item.dc:creator: Emmanuele G. Cinnirella, Andrea Nava, and Domenico Giuliano
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Quantum theory of optical spin texture in a chiral tellurium lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

Chiral tellurium exhibits a giant optical gyrotropy whose microscopic origin has remained elusive. Here, the authors develop a quantum theory revealing that this phenomenon originates from spin-textured optical waves at the atomic scale. They develop the concept of deep microscopic optical band structure to demonstrate how the twisted lattice of tellurium lifts optical degeneracies for spinning optical waves. The theory captures quantitatively the superdispersive optical gyrotropy with excellent experimental agreement, unveiling hidden optical spin features analogous to electronic spin texture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041116] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

Chiral tellurium exhibits a giant optical gyrotropy whose microscopic origin has remained elusive. Here, the authors develop a quantum theory revealing that this phenomenon originates from spin-textured optical waves at the atomic scale. They develop the concept of deep microscopic optical band structure to demonstrate how the twisted lattice of tellurium lifts optical degeneracies for spinning optical waves. The theory captures quantitatively the superdispersive optical gyrotropy with excellent experimental agreement, unveiling hidden optical spin features analogous to electronic spin texture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041116] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum theory of optical spin texture in a chiral tellurium lattice
item.dc:creator: Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

item.title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.33}{\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of Cu0.33TiSe2 across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in the normal state above the CD…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.dc:creator: G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

item.title: Uncovering surface states of the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. De Vita, J. Bakkelund, H. Świątek, M. J. Winiarski, S. Malick, C. V. B. Nielsen, F. Bertran, A. J. H. Jones, P. Majchrzak, F. Miletto Granozio, J. A. Miwa, R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli, T. Klimczuk, C. Bigi, and F. Mazzola

${\mathrm{BaMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$ is a Dirac semimetal characterized by a simple Dirac cone crossing the Fermi level at the center of the Brillouin zone, protected by ${C}_{3}$ rotational symmetry. Together with its Sr-based analog ${\mathrm{SrMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$, it has been proposed as…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035148] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. De Vita, J. Bakkelund, H. Świątek, M. J. Winiarski, S. Malick, C. V. B. Nielsen, F. Bertran, A. J. H. Jones, P. Majchrzak, F. Miletto Granozio, J. A. Miwa, R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli, T. Klimczuk, C. Bigi, and F. Mazzola

BaMg2Bi2 is a Dirac semimetal characterized by a simple Dirac cone crossing the Fermi level at the center of the Brillouin zone, protected by C3 rotational symmetry. Together with its Sr-based analog SrMg2Bi2, it has been proposed as a promising candidate for a chemically driven topological switch: …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035148] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Uncovering surface states of the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. De Vita, J. Bakkelund, H. Świątek, M. J. Winiarski, S. Malick, C. V. B. Nielsen, F. Bertran, A. J. H. Jones, P. Majchrzak, F. Miletto Granozio, J. A. Miwa, R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli, T. Klimczuk, C. Bigi, and F. Mazzola

item.title: Step defects in bismuth bromide ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{Br}}_{4}$: Spatial reconfiguration and beam splitter of helical hinge states
item.description:
Author(s): Yan-Feng Zhou, Qi-Kai Ding, Yulei Han, Qing Yan, and Qing-Feng Sun

The quasi-one-dimensional material $α\text{−}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{Br}}_{4}$ was recently predicted as a higher-order topological insulator with helical hinge states strongly dependent on the (001) surface termination. Here, we theoretically investigate how step defects on the (001) surface of $…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035435] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan-Feng Zhou, Qi-Kai Ding, Yulei Han, Qing Yan, and Qing-Feng Sun

The quasi-one-dimensional material α−Bi4Br4 was recently predicted as a higher-order topological insulator with helical hinge states strongly dependent on the (001) surface termination. Here, we theoretically investigate how step defects on the (001) surface of α−Bi4Br4 nanowires reconfigure helical…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035435] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Step defects in bismuth bromide ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{Br}}_{4}$: Spatial reconfiguration and beam splitter of helical hinge states
item.dc:creator: Yan-Feng Zhou, Qi-Kai Ding, Yulei Han, Qing Yan, and Qing-Feng Sun

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Structural investigation of pristine and Mn-intercalated flat-honeycomb bismuthene on Ag(111)
item.description:
Author(s): Ziyong Zhang, Xiaobin Chen, and Takeshi Nakagawa

Honeycomb bismuthene structures on Ag(111) were investigated using low-energy electron diffraction (LEED) and density functional theory. LEED I(V) analysis revealed that 0.5 monolayer (ML) of Bi forms an ultraflat honeycomb lattice with negligible buckling at ∼120 K, which transforms into other stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035436] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ziyong Zhang, Xiaobin Chen, and Takeshi Nakagawa

Honeycomb bismuthene structures on Ag(111) were investigated using low-energy electron diffraction (LEED) and density functional theory. LEED I(V) analysis revealed that 0.5 monolayer (ML) of Bi forms an ultraflat honeycomb lattice with negligible buckling at ∼120 K, which transforms into other stru…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035436] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Structural investigation of pristine and Mn-intercalated flat-honeycomb bismuthene on Ag(111)

item.title: ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex crystals in tetrahedral antiferromagnets: Fractional charges and topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Tomoki Hirosawa, Alexander Mook, and Maria Azhar

We report the formation of a ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex crystal in the tetrahedral antiferromagnetic order on a triangular lattice. The noncoplanar tetrahedral state consists of four sublattices with spins oriented along the faces of a tetrahedron in spin space. The long-range order characterized by …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020413] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tomoki Hirosawa, Alexander Mook, and Maria Azhar

We report the formation of a Z2 vortex crystal in the tetrahedral antiferromagnetic order on a triangular lattice. The noncoplanar tetrahedral state consists of four sublattices with spins oriented along the faces of a tetrahedron in spin space. The long-range order characterized by a Z2 topology ar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020413] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex crystals in tetrahedral antiferromagnets: Fractional charges and topological magnons
item.dc:creator: Tomoki Hirosawa, Alexander Mook, and Maria Azhar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Dynamics of surface electrons in a topological insulator: Cyclotron resonance at room temperature
item.description:
Author(s): I. Mohelsky, F. Le Mardelé, J. Dzian, J. Wyzula, X. D. Sun, C. W. Cho, B. A. Piot, M. Shankar, R. Sankar, A. Ferguson, D. Santos-Cottin, P. Marsik, C. Bernhard, A. Akrap, M. Potemski, and M. Orlita

The ability to manipulate the surface states of topological insulators using electric or magnetic fields under ambient conditions is a key step toward their integration into future electronic and optoelectronic devices. Here, we demonstrate—using cyclotron resonance measurements on a tin-doped ${\ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041201] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Mohelsky, F. Le Mardelé, J. Dzian, J. Wyzula, X. D. Sun, C. W. Cho, B. A. Piot, M. Shankar, R. Sankar, A. Ferguson, D. Santos-Cottin, P. Marsik, C. Bernhard, A. Akrap, M. Potemski, and M. Orlita

The ability to manipulate the surface states of topological insulators using electric or magnetic fields under ambient conditions is a key step toward their integration into future electronic and optoelectronic devices. Here, we demonstrate—using cyclotron resonance measurements on a tin-doped BiSbT…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041201] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026

item.title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.description:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta $\mathbf{k}$ and $−\mathbf{k}$. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multipl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta k and −k. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multiple metallic, and Mott-i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases

item.title: Dissipation-driven topological phase transitions in open quantum systems independent of system Hamiltonian
item.description:
Author(s): Tian-Shu Deng and Fan Yang

We investigate dissipation-driven topological phase transitions in one-dimensional quantum open systems governed by the Lindblad equation with linear dissipation operators, which ensure the density matrix retains its Gaussian form throughout the dynamics. By employing the modular Hamiltonian framewo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024312] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tian-Shu Deng and Fan Yang

We investigate dissipation-driven topological phase transitions in one-dimensional quantum open systems governed by the Lindblad equation with linear dissipation operators, which ensure the density matrix retains its Gaussian form throughout the dynamics. By employing the modular Hamiltonian framewo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024312] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Crossing over from flat band superconductivity to conventional superconductivity
item.description:
Author(s): M. Thumin and G. Bouzerar

Over the past ten years, flat band or geometric superconductivity has become a major issue in condensed matter physics due to the significant technological benefits it could offer. Observations of this unconventional form of superconductivity are unfortunately still very limited, and significant eff…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024513] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Thumin and G. Bouzerar

Over the past ten years, flat band or geometric superconductivity has become a major issue in condensed matter physics due to the significant technological benefits it could offer. Observations of this unconventional form of superconductivity are unfortunately still very limited, and significant eff…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024513] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Vestigial gapless boson density wave emerging between $ν=\frac{1}{2}$ fractional Chern insulator and finite-momentum supersolid
item.description:
Author(s): Hongyu Lu, Han-Qing Wu, Bin-Bin Chen, and Zi Yang Meng

The roton-triggered charge density wave (CDW) is widely studied in fractional quantum Hall (FQH) and fractional Chern insulator (FCI) systems, and there also exist field theoretical and numerical realizations of continuous transition from FCI to superfluid (SF). However, the theory and numerical exp…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035141] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hongyu Lu, Han-Qing Wu, Bin-Bin Chen, and Zi Yang Meng

The roton-triggered charge density wave (CDW) is widely studied in fractional quantum Hall (FQH) and fractional Chern insulator (FCI) systems, and there also exist field theoretical and numerical realizations of continuous transition from FCI to superfluid (SF). However, the theory and numerical exp…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035141] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: From hidden order to skyrmions: Quantum Hall states in an extended Hofstadter-Fermi-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): F. J. Pauw, U. Schollwöck, N. Goldman, S. Paeckel, and F. A. Palm

The interplay between topology and strong interactions gives rise to a variety of exotic quantum phases, including fractional quantum Hall states and their lattice analogs—fractional Chern insulators (FCIs). Such topologically ordered states host fractionalized excitations and, for spinful systems, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035142] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. J. Pauw, U. Schollwöck, N. Goldman, S. Paeckel, and F. A. Palm

The interplay between topology and strong interactions gives rise to a variety of exotic quantum phases, including fractional quantum Hall states and their lattice analogs—fractional Chern insulators (FCIs). Such topologically ordered states host fractionalized excitations and, for spinful systems, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035142] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Photoinduced excitonic magnetism in a multiorbital Hubbard system
item.description:
Author(s): Lei Geng, Sujay Ray, and Philipp Werner

Multiorbital Hubbard models with Hund coupling and crystal-field splitting exhibit an instability toward spin-triplet excitonic order in the parameter regime characterized by strong local spin fluctuations. Upon chemical doping, two distinct types of excitonic ferromagnetism have been reported. Usin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035143] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lei Geng, Sujay Ray, and Philipp Werner

Multiorbital Hubbard models with Hund coupling and crystal-field splitting exhibit an instability toward spin-triplet excitonic order in the parameter regime characterized by strong local spin fluctuations. Upon chemical doping, two distinct types of excitonic ferromagnetism have been reported. Usin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035143] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum metric induced nonlinear Edelstein effect and giant intrinsic antidamping spin-orbit torque
item.description:
Author(s): Meng-Rou Huang, Ren-Zhao Niu, Hou-Jian Duan, Ming-Xun Deng, and Rui-Qiang Wang

Antidamping-like (ADL) spin-orbit torques (SOTs) exhibit the efficiency of magnetization switching in recent experiments, especially in topological insulator/ferromagnet (TI/FM) heterostructures where a giant SOT has been reported even at room temperature. While most experiments have confirmed that …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035144] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

While most experiments have confirmed that …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035144] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Emergent topology of flat bands in a twisted bilayer $α\text{−}{T}_{3}$ lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Gourab Paul, Srijata Lahiri, Kuntal Bhattacharyya, and Saurabh Basu

We provide theoretical evidence of the emergent topology in a twisted bilayer system, despite the corresponding monolayer hosting topologically trivial properties. To this end, we investigate an interesting interplay of destructive interference due to lattice geometry and band folding due to enlarge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035145] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

To this end, we investigate an interesting interplay of destructive interference due to lattice geometry and band folding due to enlarge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035145] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident beam on a magneto-optical metagrating
item.description:
Author(s): Ma Luo

The Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident optical beam to a metagrating consisting of magneto-optical material is studied. The metagrating consists of a compound grating of magneto-optical rods atop a dielectric slab, which features staggered lateral shifts of the rods. The compound grating indu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035433] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

The Goos-Hänchen shift of a normally incident optical beam to a metagrating consisting of magneto-optical material is studied. The metagrating consists of a compound grating of magneto-optical rods atop a dielectric slab, which features staggered lateral shifts of the rods. The compound grating indu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035433] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026

item.title: Dynamical signatures and control of time-reversal symmetry breaking in twisted nodal superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Jefferson Tang and Pavel A. Volkov

Recent observations of time-reversal breaking superconductivity at twisted cuprate interfaces motivate the development of new approaches to better characterize this emergent phenomenon. Here we study the dynamical properties of the order parameters at the twisted unconventional superconductor interf…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014513] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

Recent observations of time-reversal breaking superconductivity at twisted cuprate interfaces motivate the development of new approaches to better characterize this emergent phenomenon. Here we study the dynamical properties of the order parameters at the twisted unconventional superconductor interf…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014513] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Symmetry-based real-space framework for realizing flat bands and discovering nodal-line touchings
item.description:
Author(s): Rui-Heng Liu and Xin Liu

The authors introduce here a systematic and unified real-space recipe to build flat bands from symmetric compact localized states. Multiple examples across different lattice symmetries, orbital contents and dimensions validate the versatility of the scheme. In addition, the authors derive concise criteria for determining all topological band crossings in flat-band systems — including intriguing nodal type — and demonstrate their intimate connection to underlying symmetries. The findings provide a generic theoretical framework for studying flat band models and materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035140] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rui-Heng Liu and Xin Liu

The authors introduce here a systematic and unified real-space recipe to build flat bands from symmetric compact localized states. Multiple examples across different lattice symmetries, orbital contents and dimensions validate the versatility of the scheme. In addition, the authors derive concise criteria for determining all topological band crossings in flat-band systems — including intriguing nodal type — and demonstrate their intimate connection to underlying symmetries. The findings provide a generic theoretical framework for studying flat band models and materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035140] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-based real-space framework for realizing flat bands and discovering nodal-line touchings
item.dc:creator: Rui-Heng Liu and Xin Liu

item.title: Fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes
item.description:
Author(s): Themba Hodge, Tuan Kieu, Jasmin Bedow, Eric Mascot, Dirk K. Morr, and Stephan Rachel

Braiding and fusion of Majorana zero modes are key elements of any future topological Majorana-based quantum computer. Here, we investigate the fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes in the spinless Kitaev model, as well as in a spinful model describing magnet-superconductor hybrid structures. We co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035429] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Themba Hodge, Tuan Kieu, Jasmin Bedow, Eric Mascot, Dirk K. Morr, and Stephan Rachel

Braiding and fusion of Majorana zero modes are key elements of any future topological Majorana-based quantum computer. Here, we investigate the fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes in the spinless Kitaev model, as well as in a spinful model describing magnet-superconductor hybrid structures. We co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035429] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes
item.dc:creator: Themba Hodge, Tuan Kieu, Jasmin Bedow, Eric Mascot, Dirk K. Morr, and Stephan Rachel
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Two-site Kitaev sweet spots evolving into topological islands
item.description:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Dourado, J. Carlos Egues, and Poliana H. Penteado

Here, the authors demonstrate that Majorana bound states can be identified through a simple electrical transport measurement in artificial Kitaev chains composed of quantum dots coupled by superconductors. The key result is that the non-Abelian Majorana algebra – specifically the property γ2=½ – maps directly onto conductance. When this condition is met, the conductance locks to a robust half-quantized plateau e2/2h, signaling the emergence of “topological islands” with protected Majorana modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035432] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Dourado, J. Carlos Egues, and Poliana H. Penteado

Here, the authors demonstrate that Majorana bound states can be identified through a simple electrical transport measurement in artificial Kitaev chains composed of quantum dots coupled by superconductors. The key result is that the non-Abelian Majorana algebra – specifically the property γ2=½ – maps directly onto conductance. When this condition is met, the conductance locks to a robust half-quantized plateau e2/2h, signaling the emergence of “topological islands” with protected Majorana modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035432] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-site Kitaev sweet spots evolving into topological islands
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo A. Dourado, J. Carlos Egues, and Poliana H. Penteado
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Surface topological quantum criticality II: Conformal manifolds, isolated fixed points, and entanglement
item.description:
Author(s): Saran Vijayan and Fei Zhou

In this article, we propose a possibility of realizing a distinct family of scale-conformal invariant interacting Hamiltonians in two-dimensional quantum many-body systems. Such phenomena can occur in various interacting systems, including topological surfaces or two-dimensional bulks. Based on a pr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045134] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

Such phenomena can occur in various interacting systems, including topological surfaces or two-dimensional bulks. Based on a pr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045134] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.title: Connection between free-fermion and interacting crystalline symmetry-protected topological phases
item.description:
Author(s): Chen-Shen Lee, Ken Shiozaki, and Chang-Tse Hsieh

We present a framework for investigating the effects of interactions on crystalline symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phases. Within this framework, one can establish a direct connection between the equivalence classes of free-fermion systems and their corresponding interacting classes. A central…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045135] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

Within this framework, one can establish a direct connection between the equivalence classes of free-fermion systems and their corresponding interacting classes. A central…

item.title: Electronic correlations are dominated by $c\text{−}f$ hybridization in the pyrochlore superconductor ${\mathrm{CeRu}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Suppanut Sangphet, Minyinan Lei, Xiaoxiao Wang, Xingtian Sun, Xiuhua Chen, Xin Li, Kaiwen Chen, Yilin Wang, Lei Shu, Rui Peng, Haichao Xu, and Donglai Feng

Flat bands near the Fermi energy (${E}_{\mathrm{F}}$) enhance the density of states and promote strong electronic correlations, often driving novel quantum phases. Understanding the microscopic origin of these correlations is essential for uncovering the mechanisms behind correlated phenomena such a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045136] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Suppanut Sangphet, Minyinan Lei, Xiaoxiao Wang, Xingtian Sun, Xiuhua Chen, Xin Li, Kaiwen Chen, Yilin Wang, Lei Shu, Rui Peng, Haichao Xu, and Donglai Feng

Flat bands near the Fermi energy (EF) enhance the density of states and promote strong electronic correlations, often driving novel quantum phases. Understanding the microscopic origin of these correlations is essential for uncovering the mechanisms behind correlated phenomena such as superconductiv…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045136] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic correlations are dominated by $c\text{−}f$ hybridization in the pyrochlore superconductor ${\mathrm{CeRu}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Suppanut Sangphet, Minyinan Lei, Xiaoxiao Wang, Xingtian Sun, Xiuhua Chen, Xin Li, Kaiwen Chen, Yilin Wang, Lei Shu, Rui Peng, Haichao Xu, and Donglai Feng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Collapsing of spin-split Fermi surfaces in the magnetic Weyl semimetal candidate EuAuSb
item.description:
Author(s): Juntao Yao, Zhixiang Hu, Niraj Aryal, Asish K. Kundu, Antu Laha, Chuhang Liu, Lijun Wu, Yimei Zhu, Elio Vescovo, Weiguo Yin, and Qiang Li

Strong coupling between magnetic moments and itinerant electrons can drive topological phase transitions and Fermi surface reconstructions, offering promising routes toward topological quantum computing. Here, the authors report a rare instance of Fermi surface splitting and collapse in the magnetic Weyl semimetal EuAuSb, revealed by temperature dependent Shubnikov–de Haas quantum oscillation measurements. These phenomena are attributed to the progressive weakening of short-range magnetic correlations and the exchange coupling between conduction electrons and localized magnetic moments with increasing temperature.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045138] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Juntao Yao, Zhixiang Hu, Niraj Aryal, Asish K. Kundu, Antu Laha, Chuhang Liu, Lijun Wu, Yimei Zhu, Elio Vescovo, Weiguo Yin, and Qiang Li

Strong coupling between magnetic moments and itinerant electrons can drive topological phase transitions and Fermi surface reconstructions, offering promising routes toward topological quantum computing. Here, the authors report a rare instance of Fermi surface splitting and collapse in the magnetic Weyl semimetal EuAuSb, revealed by temperature dependent Shubnikov–de Haas quantum oscillation measurements. These phenomena are attributed to the progressive weakening of short-range magnetic correlations and the exchange coupling between conduction electrons and localized magnetic moments with increasing temperature.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045138] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Collapsing of spin-split Fermi surfaces in the magnetic Weyl semimetal candidate EuAuSb
item.dc:creator: Juntao Yao, Zhixiang Hu, Niraj Aryal, Asish K. Kundu, Antu Laha, Chuhang Liu, Lijun Wu, Yimei Zhu, Elio Vescovo, Weiguo Yin, and Qiang Li

item.title: Nonequilibrium statistics of a biased Kondo resonance
item.description:
Author(s): Jong E. Han

Numerical renormalization group (NRG) is formulated for nonequilibrium steady state by converting finite-lattice many-body eigenstates into scattering states. Extension of the full-density-matrix NRG for a biased Anderson impurity model, simplified by using the original orbital basis of the Hamilton…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045141] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

Extension of the full-density-matrix NRG for a biased Anderson impurity model, simplified by using the original orbital basis of the Hamilton…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045141] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.description:
Author(s): Hao He, Zhao Gong, Shuai Li, Jian-Jun Liu, Hui-Ying Mu, and Xing-Tao An

Recent studies have demonstrated that topological quantum spin Hall (QSH) states exist in twisted bilayers of transition metal dichalcogenides. However, a comprehensive theoretical characterization of the topological edge states remains a topic of interest and an unresolved issue. Here, the topologi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045423] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

However, a comprehensive theoretical characterization of the topological edge states remains a topic of interest and an unresolved issue. Here, the topologi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045423] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
